response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with strong web, backend, and low-latency application skills, but his background is almost entirely disconnected from the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload performance analysis — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant experience is writing low-latency DLang applications at Sociomantic and some embedded/robotics work at Altom, but these fall far short of the hardware-level, chip-architecture focus required here.
strengths: Over 14 years of software engineering experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years educational path requirement
Demonstrated low-latency and performance-oriented systems work (async I/O, caching, high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic)
Broad polyglot background including C/C++ and embedded systems exposure, showing adaptability beyond web-only work
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is the central technical requirement of the role
No background in AI/ML workloads, deep neural network optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD systems
Current career trajectory (web full-stack, TypeScript, Node.js, MCP tooling) moves further away from hardware-level performance architecture, not toward it

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU workload expertise
Low-latency systems and performance optimization background
